methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Harbinger
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Candela is a Series C transportation company that has raised €129M+ to date. The valuation universe consists of 7 private peers in the transportation sector, including Einride, Harbinger, Rivian, The Boring Company, and REGENT Craft. No public comparables exist in this subsector, limiting traditional market-based anchors.

Peer-median valuation analysis is not applicable here. The transportation sector contains only 5 same-sector peers, but filtering for matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier yields fewer than three datapoints in any single cohort—below the three-company minimum required for statistical reliability. Applying all-sector medians would introduce material bias given the heterogeneous business models, capital intensity, and development timelines across transportation subsegments.

The absence of a sufficiently dense comparable set reflects the nascent nature of this subsector. Valuation of Candela therefore cannot be reliably supported by peer multiples alone. Alternative methods—DCF, precedent transactions, or venture equity method applied to the private peer set with explicit adjustments for stage and capital efficiency—would be necessary to triangulate a defensible range. The key uncertainty is whether peer valuations themselves reflect genuine market pricing or remain anchored to earlier fundraising rounds. Without additional data on comparable transaction multiples or detailed peer financial performance, a point estimate remains unsupported.
